user_query:
Run a lightweight network health check for my workstation. Read input/targets.json for the list of hosts to test. Please:

- Check interface status and RX/TX totals.
- List listening TCP ports.
- Estimate bandwidth over about 5 seconds.
- For each host in input/targets.json: run a short latency test (around 5 pings), a hop-limited path trace (cap ~15 hops), a DNS lookup, and WHOIS for domain-type hosts only.
- Attempt a small download speed test.

Deliver:
- A concise report saved to output/network_report.md with the following sections in this exact order: Interfaces, Listening Ports, Bandwidth, Latency Tests, Traceroutes, DNS Results, WHOIS Summaries, Speed Test, Recommendations. Include brief, plain-English notes and call out any failures/timeouts.
- A machine-readable summary saved to output/metrics.json with fields: tested_hosts (array), timestamp (ISO string), interfaces (array), latency (object keyed by host with attempted/success/avg_ms), dns (object keyed by host with an array of IPs), traceroute (object keyed by host with attempted/success and hop_count or null), whois (object keyed by domain hosts with attempted/success and registrar string if present), bandwidth (object with per-interface rx_kbps/tx_kbps from your measurement), speed_test (object with attempted/success and mbps or null).
- A reproducibility log saved to output/commands_run.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with fields cmd, target (if any), and status ("attempted", "success", or "failed"). Record one entry for each action above (status/ports/bandwidth/speed) and, for every host listed, one entry each for latency, trace, and dns; include whois only for domain hosts. If something times out or lacks permissions, mark it appropriately and continue.

Use brief timeouts and do not block for more than ~30 seconds total. Keep the report readable and include short recommendations, e.g., if latency is high or DNS fails. Write only to the specified output/ paths.
